prompt:
The lunchroom Connect Four ladder is moving onto our chat bot and I need the engine plus a house bot whose play is exactly reproducible — we archive games as move lists and replay them during disputes, so the bot must be a pure function of (position, depth). Build connect4.ts (stdlib only, no npm); test_connect4.ts is the acceptance contract, hands off.

Standard 7-column, 6-row board. Game state is plain data { board, turn, winner }: board is 6 rows of 7 cells, row 0 the TOP, '.' empty, 'X' and 'O' the players, X always moves first; winner is 'X', 'O', 'draw' or null. Export ROWS and COLS too. newGame() gives the empty position. drop(game, col) is pure (never touches its argument): the piece lands on the lowest free row of that column, the turn flips, and winner is set when the drop makes four in a row horizontally, vertically, or on either diagonal — or 'draw' when the board fills without one. A column outside 0..6 throws RangeError; dropping into a full column or a finished game throws Error. legalMoves(game) lists the playable columns ascending, and is empty once the game is over.

parseBoard(text) loads fixture positions: exactly 6 lines of 7 characters from '.XO' (a trailing newline is fine), rejecting wrong shapes, unknown characters, floating pieces (a piece with an empty cell below it), impossible counts (O ahead of X, or X ahead by 2+), and boards where both sides have four in a row. The side to move comes from the counts (equal means X). If the drawn position already contains a four-in-a-row, winner reflects it; a full board with no line is a 'draw'. render(game) returns the 6 rows joined with \n, top row first, no trailing newline.

The bot: bestMove(game, depth) searches EXACTLY depth plies of plain negamax — no pruning, no move ordering tricks. Position value for the side to move: 0 for a draw; a position where the side to move has already lost is worth -(10000 - ply) counting plies from the search root, so a faster win outranks a slower one; at depth 0 the value is the leaf heuristic = (own discs in the center column) minus (opponent discs in the center column). Candidate columns are scanned in ascending order and only a STRICTLY better value replaces the current champion, so ties break to the lowest column — this is load-bearing, the tests pin exact bot moves. depth < 1 throws RangeError, a finished game throws Error.

selfPlay(depth) plays the bot against itself from the empty board until the game ends and returns { moves, game }. The suite pins full transcripts: depth 1 is a 15-move X win, depth 2 fills all 42 cells to a draw, depth 3 is an 18-move O win — if any of those differ, some part of the search law above isn't exact.

node --test test_connect4.ts must pass as shipped.
